page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is an x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon CPU achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1851.77 and a multi-core score of 15448.63, supported by AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 842.59 GB/sec and an uncached large block write bandwidth of 57.5 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance delivers 15.61 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 16.35 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

The primary trade-off of the ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications or large-scale database systems. However, this resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation without massive memory overhead. It is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference, where it manages 247.77 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the middle 50% tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 19335.86 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.61 million operations per second. The low memory allocation per core represents a clear trade-off, making it less suitable for memory-heavy databases but highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is a KVM-based server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for compute-bound workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge features 16 Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps bandwidth, offering balanced mid-tier compute performance.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core compute instance with a lean memory profile, optimized for CPU-bound tasks and static web serving.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 1.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
Mid-tier performance with 15.61 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and text processing
